quote: note="arki is the suppletive plural of ek 'come'; 3rd-person subjects have zero person-marking, so the verb appears without a prefix. utar 'people' is the formal-noun head of the headless relative clause."

quote: note="The dative/directional postposition eun 'to, about' remains inside the relative clause; usi fills the gap as the headless place-head. quote: note="Classical Ainu (Itadori/Ishikari data). The bare concept form hum 'sound' — not the possessed hum-i — takes the preceding clause as its content. Shibatani reads this as a construction intermediate between a relative clause and an evidential nominalization."

quote: note="The bare hum 'sound/sensation' (not the possessed hum-i) combined with the existential as 'stand/exist'. Bugaeva reads this as an emerging GNMCC pattern in which hum classifies the type of sensation rather than heading a gapped external relative clause. Dal Corso 2020: 52 cites the same distributional contrast in his structural analysis."
